title: About
text:
Hi, I’m Shalini Harwalkar, a Master’s student in Business Analytics at UC San Diego with a background in Computer Science and Engineering.
I’m passionate about using data to solve real-world problems, especially when it involves turning messy datasets into clear, actionable insights. My interests lie in data science, statistical modeling, and machine learning, and I enjoy working on projects that combine technical depth with practical impact.

title: Car Performance Analysis
text:
Research Question
How does engine displacement relate to fuel efficiency?

title: Car Performance Analysis
text:
Dataset
We use the mpg dataset, which includes: - miles per gallon - displacement - horsepower - origin - model year

title: Car Performance Analysis
text:
Visualization
#| echo: false #| warning: false #| message: false
import seaborn as sns import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = sns.load_dataset(“mpg”).dropna()
plt.figure() sns.scatterplot(x=“mpg”, y=“displacement”, hue=“origin”, size=“horsepower”, data=df) plt.title(“Fuel Efficiency vs Engine Displacement”) plt.xlabel(“Miles per Gallon”) plt.ylabel(“Engine Displacement”) plt.show()

title: Analysis of Student Performance
text: This analysis explores how study habits and attendance influence student performance. Understanding these relationships can help identify key factors that drive academic success.

title: Analysis of Student Performance
text:
This analysis explores the relationship between study habits and student performance.
Here is a plot:

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(42)

n = 150
study_hours = np.random.randint(1, 10, n)
attendance = np.random.randint(50, 100, n)

score = 5 * study_hours + 0.5 * attendance + np.random.normal(0, 5, n)

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "study_hours": study_hours,
    "attendance": attendance,
    "score": score
})

plt.scatter(df["study_hours"], df["score"])
plt.xlabel("Study Hours")
plt.ylabel("Final Score")
plt.title("Study Hours vs Performance")
plt.show()

title: Analysis of Student Performance
text: This analysis explores how study habits and attendance influence student performance. Understanding these relationships can help identify key factors that drive academic success.

title: Analysis of Student Performance
text:
Study Hours vs Performance
The scatter plot below shows the relationship between study hours and final exam scores.

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(42)

n = 150
study_hours = np.random.randint(1, 10, n)
attendance = np.random.randint(50, 100, n)

score = 5 * study_hours + 0.5 * attendance + np.random.normal(0, 5, n)

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "study_hours": study_hours,
    "attendance": attendance,
    "score": score
})

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["study_hours"], df["score"])
plt.xlabel("Study Hours")
plt.ylabel("Final Score")
plt.title("Study Hours vs Performance")
plt.show()
